items.title: Petra Energy unit wins two-year Sarawak PSC contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petra Energy Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary has secured a contract to provide field operations management services for an offshore production sharing contract (PSC) in Sarawak. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said Petra Energy Development Sdn Bhd had received a letter of award from Vestigo Petroleum Sdn Bhd for the SK407 PSC and its associated facilities. The SK407 field is located offshore Miri, Sarawak, and comprises five offshore fields, namely West Lutong, Baram, Tukau, Siwa and Fairly Baram.

items.title: Rescued kidnapping victim turns out to be Interpol Red Notice subject
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have rescued a kidnapped South Korean man, only to later discover that he is wanted in his home country. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the 40-year-old was abducted while travelling from Genting Highlands on April 17 before being rescued at a homestay in Melaka. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Kota Kinabalu man gets 15 months in jail for car theft disguised as purchase
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 24 — A 25-year-old man was sentenced to 15 months’ jail and fined RM2,000 or three months’ jail by the Magistrate’s Court here for stealing a car under the pretext of buying it. Aedan Dylan John pleaded guilty before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus to stealing a Perodua Kenari EZ (Auto) from one Soong Che Wai, at about 1.30pm on March 30, this year, at a taxi parking area of the Kota Kinabalu International Airport. Police arrested Aedan on April 3, at 4.30pm in Sipitang.

items.title: Australian woman found safe after Mount Santubong hiking ordeal
items.summary: KUCHING: An Australian woman has been rescued after spending a night in a deep ravine on Mount Santubong in Sarawak after straying from a hiking trail. Kuching police chief Assistant Commissioner Alexson Naga Chabu said the victim, Belinda Lapier, in her 40s, was located by rescue teams in a ravine about 700m deep at 11.59am. Alexson said 24 personnel, including firefighters, police, the Malaysian armed forces, Sarawak Forestry Department rangers and members of the public, were involved in the search and rescue operation.

items.title: Murder probe opened over fatal drink-driving crash involving soldier on MEX
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened a murder investigation into a military personnel arrested over suspected drink-driving following a fatal crash on the Maju Expressway in Kuala Lumpur early yesterday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the suspect was brought to the Dang Wangi district police headquarters today, where an application for remand was made to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code. More to come ... © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RM230mil NGO fund probe: MACC tracing money flow from banks, says Azam
items.summary: NILAI: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is tracing the flow of funds in and out of accounts to complete investigations into the alleged misappropriation of about RM230 million involving a non-governmental organisation (NGO). MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the commission was obtaining the necessary information from financial institutions and expects to receive the records within a week. He said the bank statements would be crucial in helping investigators complete the probe.

items.title: Six shots fired in Johor killings, suspect to be charged Monday
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: At least six shots are believed to have been fired in a shooting at a restaurant in Taman Kota Jaya on April 19, which left three people dead. Johor police chief Ab Rahaman Arsad said investigators recovered six bullet casings at the scene. He said the 71-year-old suspect is believed to have acted over a debt dispute involving RM50,000 with one of the victims, a 37-year-old Vietnamese woman.

items.title: Selangor records 209 water theft cases last year, most involving commercial accounts
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 24 — A total of 209 water theft cases were reported in Selangor last year, with most involving commercial accounts, the State Legislative Assembly was told today. State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said 184 of the cases involved commercial accounts, while the remaining 25 involved domestic accounts. He said 43 water theft cases had been recorded as of March this year, involving 12 domestic accounts and 31 commercial accounts.

items.title: Police detain foreigner in Niah after threat to kill sister, seize machete and homemade shotgun
items.summary: MIRI, April 24 — Police have arrested a 37-year-old foreign man for allegedly threatening to kill his younger sister with a machete in their home. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) received a report at 1.44pm on April 22 from the 35-year-old sister. Mohd Farhan said the suspect has been remanded for four days until April 27 to assist in investigations.

items.title: May 14 decision on Zahid's bid for acquittal in Yayasan Akalbudi case
items.summary: High Court Judge Nurulhuda Nur'aini Mohamad Nor fixed the date after counsel Datuk Hamidi Mohd Noh, representing Zahid, informed the court that the defence had applied to vacate today's decision date pending the Court of Appeal's ruling on May 7. "The court sets May 14 for the decision, subject to the outcome of the Court of Appeal on May 7," she said. The Bar filed an appeal against the High Court's decision to reject the application for leave to appeal to challenge the Attorney General's (AG) decision discontinuing proceedings in Zahid's case.

items.title: Hospital staff remanded over RM54,000 false claims probe
items.summary: KUANTAN: A hospital staff member has been remanded in connection with a probe into false claims involving surgical equipment worth about RM54,000. Magistrate Siti Aisyah Ahmad issued the remand order. The first suspect was detained on Tuesday.

items.title: Microsoft targets 800,000 Malaysians for AI upskilling
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Microsoft aims to equip 800,000 Malaysians with AI skills through the AIForMYFuture initiative, building upon the Microsoft Elevate programme. Supported by the National AI Office and the National TVET Council, the initiative supports Malaysia's ambition to become a regional AI hub by 2030 by focusing on awareness, access, and adoption across all sectors of society. The launch of Microsoft Elevate marks the expansion of a national AI capacity building initiative aimed at strengthening Malaysia's readiness for AI, in line with the country's ambition to become an AI nation by 2030.

items.title: Penang CM urges cloud seeding as critically low Sungai Muda levels threaten state water supply
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang state government hopes cloud seeding operations can be carried out in upstream Kedah to raise water levels in Sungai Muda. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the measure was important to ensure sufficient raw water supply in the state. Chow said the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) had contacted authorities in Kedah to coordinate immediate action.

items.title: Police arrest three teenagers in Tawau over alleged rape of 15-year-old schoolgirl
items.summary: District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the suspects, including the main suspect, were detained at about 6.50pm on Wednesday at the Merotai police outpost. “Police moved swiftly to locate the suspects following the report,” he said yesterday. Police also detained two other suspects believed to have been involved in the incident.

items.title: Bring them home: Protesters demand return of elephants from Japan
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A group of animal lovers today called for the return of three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat — from Japan, citing concerns over their wellbeing following their relocation last month. About 20 demonstrators, led by the Sekretariat Bahana Kesetiaan Negara, held a peaceful gathering at the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry headquarters, where they submitted a memorandum urging the authorities to bring the elephants home. Social activist Kalai Vanar, who represented the group, said the memorandum was received by ministry officials, including the ministry's deputy secretary-general Datuk Mas Rizal Mohd Hilmi and Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) deputy director-general (conservation) Datuk Fakhrul Hatta Musa.

items.title: Anwar: List of MACC chief candidates submitted to King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A list of candidates for the post of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner has been presented to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for consideration, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Mother seeks RM5k for son's urgent surgery after crash
items.summary: Mohamad Amirullah Mohd Shaferi sustained severe injuries, including head trauma, a fractured jaw and a broken left shoulder. His mother, Zabariah Hassan, said Amirullah had recently undergone surgery to remove blood clots and treat a head infection, and would require further procedures, resulting in significant costs. Zabariah said her son urgently needs jaw surgery, but the family is struggling to afford it.

items.title: Bukit Aman CID: Bangsar’s Jalan Telawi shooting tied to revenge over past gang turf disputes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A shooting along Jalan Telawi in Bangsar on April 22 is believed to have stemmed from revenge linked to past territorial disputes between organised crime groups, police have confirmed. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said a 41-year-old local man was shot in the abdomen following an altercation between two groups outside an entertainment outlet during the incident. The victim’s condition is stable, Kumar said, adding that the two suspects arrested over the incident are currently in remand.

items.title: High Court sets May 14 for decision on Zahid’s bid for acquittal in Akalbudi case
items.summary: High Court Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor fixed the date after counsel Datuk Hamidi Mohd Noh, representing Ahmad Zahid, informed the court that the defence had applied to vacate today’s decision date pending the Court of Appeal’s ruling on May 7. “The court sets May 14 for the decision, subject to the outcome of the Court of Appeal on May 7,” she said. Earlier, Hamidi said that on April 20, the defence had filed a letter seeking to vacate today’s decision date pending the appellate court’s ruling.

items.title: Selangor to adopt Year One entry at age six from 2027, hire teachers for influx
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 24 — The Selangor state government will adopt the Education Ministry's Year One pupil admission policy starting at age six for the 2027 school session, including primary schools handled by the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (JAIS). Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari informed the state assembly today that plans are in place to hire more teachers and upgrade school facilities to meet the influx of Year One students. “Secondly, coordinating student data to pinpoint densely populated areas alongside the Selangor State Education Department.

items.title: Post-mortem completed for Bangladeshi tourist killed in MEX crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The post-mortem examination on Bangladeshi tourist Muzahid Millad, who was killed in a suspected drink-driving crash early yesterday, has been completed. "The post-mortem was completed yesterday," the spokesperson said at Kuala Lumpur Hospital today. His wife, Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, 23, was injured and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital.

items.title: Terengganu schools halt outdoor activities amid hot, dry spell
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: All schools in Terengganu have been instructed to suspend outdoor activities due to the ongoing hot and dry weather. Terengganu State Education Department director Kamsah @ Kamal Mohamed said a directive had been issued to all schools and took effect from April 20. "All outdoor activities have been cancelled, including water-based activities, while indoor activities may proceed.

items.title: Penang LRT Mutiara Line construction on track, says CM amid political interference claims
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang LRT Mutiara Line project remains on schedule despite the tender process for Package Two still pending finalisation. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said works on Package One are ongoing, dismissing claims that political interference had caused delays. Asked about reports of political interference in the tender process for Package Two, Chow Kon Yeow said the state government was not involved, as the process is handled by project owner MRT Corp.

items.title: Careless lane change leads to motorcycle rear-end collision
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: A viral dashcam video showing a motorcycle being rear-ended by a car along Jalan 16/11 here has prompted police to open an investigation into the incident. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsuddin Mamat said the incident occurred at 7.10am yesterday, while a report was lodged at 3pm. He said the motorcycle, ridden by the victim with a pillion passenger, was travelling in the left lane before suddenly moving into the right lane.

items.title: Trial over Genting founder's late daughter inheritance dispute adjourned to June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today postponed the trial over the Genting inheritance dispute involving the late Lim Siew Kim's will and her two daughters to June. Lawyer Datuk Seri V Sithambaram, representing the siblings, requested a deferral, citing time constraints as the court was engaged in another ongoing trial. Sithambaram represents Chan T'shiao Li, 48, and Kimberly Chan Tshiao Miin, 45, who are contesting their late mother's will involving assets worth more than RM1.6bn.

items.title: Iran fast-boat swarms add to Hormuz threats for shipping
items.summary: LONDON: Iran's use of a swarm of small, fast boats to seize two container ships near the Strait of Hormuz could undermine suggestions US forces have disabled its naval threat and reveals the challenges facing reopening one of the world's most important oil export routes. US President Donald Trump on Monday acknowledged that while Iran's conventional navy had been largely destroyed, its "fast-attack ships" had not been considered much of a threat. Some 100 or more may have been destroyed since the Iran war began of Feb 28, said Corey Ranslem, chief executive of maritime security group Dryad Global.

items.title: Penang cements role as Malaysia’s automation, test and equipment hub with launch of collaborative campus initiative
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — Penang is strengthening its position as a key hub for automation, test and equipment (ATE) within Malaysia’s semiconductor industry with the launch of a new industry-led ATE campus. Penang chief minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state has the highest concentration of ATE companies and ecosystem partners in the country. “Within this ecosystem, Penang hosts more than 20 local ATE companies and over 80 automation, precision engineering, and component firms,” he said in his opening address at the launch of the ATE Campus in Olive Tree Hotel here.

items.title: China's DeepSeek says releases long-awaited new AI model
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese startup DeepSeek released a new artificial intelligence (AI) model Friday, more than a year after it stunned the world with a low-cost reasoning model that matched the capabilities of US rivals. Reports said Microsoft was also looking to trim its ranks. A "preview version" of the open source model is now available, the company said.

items.title: Gold set for weekly drop as higher oil prices stoke inflation fears
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Friday and were on track for a weekly drop, as elevated oil prices fuelled fears of inflation and higher-for-longer interest rates amid stalled US-Iran peace talks. Spot gold was down 0.7 per cent at US$4,661.33 per ounce, as of 0426 GMT. US gold futures for June delivery fell one per cent to US$4,676.50.

items.title: King Charles to visit US as political ties fray under Trump
items.summary: LONDON: King Charles III heads to the United States on Monday with transatlantic tensions over the Iran war and the Epstein scandal's long shadow threatening to intrude on the landmark visit. Both Buckingham Palace and the British government have said the four-day trip will honour the historic relationship between the two countries as the US marks 250 years of independence. Charles's first US state visit as monarch comes at the request of the UK government and President Donald Trump and will be made with Queen Camilla, according to the palace.

items.title: Terengganu schools suspend outdoor activities amid hot weather
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 24 — All schools in Terengganu have been instructed to suspend outdoor activities due to the current hot and dry weather. Terengganu State Education Department (JPNT) director Kamsah@Kamal Mohamed said the notification on the matter had been distributed to all schools in the state, and was effective from April 20. “All outdoor activities have been cancelled, including water-based activities, but indoor activities are permitted.

items.title: Over 5,000 litres of diesel seized in Sarikei raid
items.summary: SARIKEI: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry seized 5,030 litres of diesel during an enforcement operation at a premises in Sungai Mador, Bintangor, yesterday. "Inspections found that 5,030 litres of diesel were stored at the location without valid authorisation from the Controller of Supplies," he said in a statement. He said a lorry, six intermediate bulk containers (IBC), 17 jerry cans, three electric pumps and eight rubber hoses were also seized.

items.title: ‘Can’t remain comfortable’: Anwar again urges private sector to adopt flexible work, WFH amid fuel cost pressures
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today called on the private sector to consider implementing flexible work arrangements similar to those practised in the public sector, including work-from-home (WFH). Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the move could help reduce fuel consumption, ease cost pressures and maintain workforce productivity and morale. Anwar said the government has also provided the Madani Economy framework and clear policies to elevate the nation’s standing, and as such the private sector must fully seize the space and opportunities created by these reform efforts.

items.title: Home Ministry to engage publishers before acting on contentious titles
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry will strengthen its engagement with the publishing industry, including holding preliminary discussions with publishers before taking action against their publications. The ministry said the move followed an engagement session with the Malaysian Book Publishers Association on April 21, where both sides discussed current issues and developments in the industry. However, the ministry stressed that all publications must comply with legal provisions and must not contain elements that could jeopardise public safety, harmony and well-being.

items.title: Man arrested after smashing Perodua Bezza window with stone at Ipoh night market
items.summary: IPOH, April 24 — Police arrested a local man suspected of smashing the front passenger window of a Perodua Bezza with a stone after a dispute over car polishing fees on Wednesday. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said officers received a report of vehicle damage at the Gerbang Malam night market on April 21. “Acting on intelligence, police arrested a 43-year-old local man around 1am on April 22.

items.title: Zeekr expands flagship SUV line-up with 8X
items.summary: ZEEKR, a luxury technology brand under Geely Auto Group, has unveiled a new high-performance flagship SUV aimed at expanding its premium vehicle portfolio. The 8X model joins the Zeekr 9X to form the brand's dual-flagship SUV lineup. "As a flagship five-seater, the Zeekr 8X shatters the conventional notion that 'performance and comfort are mutually exclusive' with MPV-like spaciousness, one-touch comfort of the Eames lounge chair and the world's only Naim audio system, offering a new luxury choice that balances driving performance with comfort," Zeekr said.

items.title: KTMB rolls out special Aidiladha train to East Coast
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) will operate a special 'Ekspres Lambaian Aidiladha' service to the East Coast to meet balik kampung demand ahead of Hari Raya Aidiladha next month. In a statement today, KTMB said the service will comprise two trips, with tickets going on sale from 10am on April 24. The return service will depart Tumpat at 8.30pm on May 29 and arrive at KL Sentral at 2.55pm the next day.

items.title: Kedah raids expose illegal shops run by foreigners
items.summary: They were found running premises such as car wash outlets, barber shops, bicycle stores and budget hotels. Their activities were uncovered by the Kedah Immigration Department, which detained 10 foreigners during a series of operations — Ops Gegar, Ops Mahir, Ops Dandan, Ops Minyak and Ops Sapu — conducted in the two districts on Wednesday. [[nid:1424970]] A total of six premises were inspected, including car wash outlets, barber shops, bicycle stores and budget hotels.

items.title: Court upholds RM825,000 defamation award to three DAP leaders against Siti Mastura over Chin Peng claim
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 24 — The Court of Appeal here today dismissed the appeal by Kepala Batas MP Dr Siti Mastura Muhammad against the Penang High Court’s decision ordering her to pay damages, including costs, totalling RM825,000 for defaming three DAP leaders by linking them to Communist Party of Malaya leader, Chin Peng. Delivering the unanimous decision, Justice Ahmad Kamal said the appellate court affirmed the entirety of the High Court’s judgment delivered on Dec 4, 2024, including the award of global damages and costs totalling RM825,000. Justice Ahmad Kamal, who delivered today’s decision via Zoom, also said the court found no merit in the plaintiff’s cross-appeal (DAP leaders).

items.title: Sungai Muda hits danger level, Penang activates contingency plan
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang has averted a potential water supply disruption after the Sungai Muda river level plunged to a "danger level" of 1.27m at the Lahar Tiang intake yesterday. Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the sharp drop was recorded about 4.30pm, well below the safe threshold of 2.0m. "In response, we activated the Sungai Muda Contingency Plan and released about 300 MLD from the Expanded Mengkuang Dam (EMD) to sustain operations at the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant (WTP)," he said today.

items.title: Firefighters take over 10 hours to extinguish blaze at Penang recycling plant
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department deputy fire operations director John Sagun Francis said they received the emergency call at 1.54pm, and the first crew from Bukit Mertajam fire station rushed to the scene. “Upon arrival, the team found the fire engulfing a pile of paper waste and cardboard in an open factory area spanning an estimated 3,716.12 square metres. The operation also had support from Sungai Bakap, Batu Kawan, and Bandar Perda fire stations, 11 volunteer fire brigades, and an excavator to break up the burning waste piles.

items.title: Foton to expand Malaysia push with V9 and V7 trucks
items.summary: CHINA's Foton is set to expand its presence in Malaysia with the introduction of two new pickup truck models, marking the brand's latest push into the increasingly competitive lifestyle and utility vehicle segment. The new Tunland V9 and Tunland V7 models are powered by a 2.0-litre turbo diesel mild-hybrid (MHEV) powertrain producing 161PS and 450Nm of torque, combining stronger acceleration with lower emissions. Maximum estimated price for Tunland V9 is RM148,888 before official price launch, while Tunland V7 is estimated at a maximum RM128,888 (before official price launch).

items.title: India criticises 'poor taste' Trump post against immigrants
items.summary: WASHINGTON: India on Thursday criticised as inappropriate a post by US President Donald Trump that called the South Asian country a "hellhole." Trump late Wednesday posted on social media a screed apparently written by someone else denouncing the US constitutional right to citizenship of everyone born in the country. The post accused Indian immigrants in the tech industry of not hiring white native-born Americans and inaccurately alleged that Indian immigrants lack English proficiency.

items.title: Architecture graduate turns TV repairman to support family of six
items.summary: KAJANG: It never crossed Khairul Anuar Ya'akub's mind that he would one day earn a living repairing televisions. Today, Khairul Anuar is able to support his wife, Nur Suhana Roslan, 40, and their four children aged between 8 and 16. I even wondered if anyone would want television repair services," he said.

items.title: Police trace serial number of M16 rifle seized in Kelantan raid amid suspected smuggling
items.summary: TANAH MERAH, April 24 — An M16 rifle discovered during a raid in Kampung Miak, Gua Musang, last Monday is currently undergoing forensic examination to determine whether it belongs to national security forces or was smuggled in from a neighbouring country. Kelantan Police Chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said a detailed investigation is being carried out by the police, including efforts to trace the serial number of the weapon, which has been classified as an unusual find. If checks confirm it does not belong to local security agencies, it is highly likely to have been smuggled from abroad,” he said at a press conference at the Tanah Merah District Police Headquarters here yesterday.

items.title: Fomca: Regulate e-commerce returns to curb abuse, protect sellers
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The government should regulate e-commerce platforms offering return and refund systems to prevent abuse that leaves sellers bearing losses, the Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations (Fomca) said. Its operations director, Nur Asyikin Aminuddin, said the issue is particularly worrying as sellers face improper returns, including empty boxes or items that do not match original orders. Nur Asyikin said consumers are entitled to refunds within a reasonable period, provided returns are made legitimately.

items.title: Autism card registrations surge nearly fourfold since 2021 as Malaysian awareness grows, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Registrations for the Autism Persons with Disabilities (PwD) card have risen to nearly 89,000 this year from about 23,000 in 2021, reflecting growing awareness, earlier detection and greater public acceptance, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. He said the almost fourfold increase showed that more parents were recognising early signs of autism and seeking professional diagnosis for their children. Dzulkefly said the ministry, together with the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry, would also continue strengthening Community-Based Rehabilitation Centres to ensure no one was left behind.

items.title: Man held over father’s death in Kota Bharu confirmed to have schizophrenia, says Kelantan top cop
items.summary: Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said preliminary investigations found the incident occurred when the 83-year-old victim reprimanded the 41-year-old suspect while he was relieving himself. “After the incident, the victim again reprimanded the suspect, who appeared confused before going upstairs to sleep,” he said. Police yesterday confirmed that the victim later died from injuries sustained in the 11.30am incident.

items.title: Malaysia records historic RM203.99b tax collection in 2025, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Malaysia recorded its highest-ever tax revenue collection of RM203.99 billion in 2025, marking a 10.38 per cent increase year-on-year, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said today. “The success of HASiL in achieving tax collection exceeding RM200 billion for the first time in history — at RM203.991 billion — with an increase of RM19.186 billion or 10.38 per cent compared to the previous year is extraordinary,” he said. He added that the performance allowed the government to address longstanding obligations, including settling RM11.4 billion in outstanding tax refunds owed to companies.

items.title: Bangladeshi man killed in MEX crash identified as popular gaming YouTuber, fans call for justice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A Bangladeshi man killed in a crash believed to have been caused by a drunk driver on the Maju Expressway yesterday has been identified as gaming content creator Advance Gaming. His wife, 23-year-old Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, was injured and is receiving treatment at Hospital Kuala Lumpur. On social media, Muzahid’s death sparked an outpouring of grief among Bangladesh’s online gaming community, with hashtags including #JusticeForMuzahid, #JusticeForAdvanceGaming and #BangladeshDemandsJustice trending.

items.title: MACC seizes RM18.36m in properties in NGO fund probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has seized immovable assets worth an estimated RM18.36 million involving 14 properties as part of an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of funds by a non-governmental organisation (NGO). In a TikTok post, the MACC said the seizure includes seven residential properties valued at RM15.53 million, among them a bungalow worth RM10 million. It also involves three office units valued at RM1.53 million and four plots of land worth RM1.3 million located in Kundang, Marang and Temasya Glenmarie.

items.title: Azam Baki: MACC officers must declare NGO ties to prevent conflicts of interest
items.summary: Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the move is crucial to ensure the agency’s reputation and credibility are consistently safeguarded, particularly in situations involving misappropriation cases that could undermine public trust. He said all MACC divisions and state offices must optimise resource usage, including electricity and water, regulate fuel consumption and manage departmental vehicles more efficiently to ensure effective public spending. He said the strategy encompasses effective enforcement, comprehensive corruption prevention and efficient organisational management.

items.title: Malaysia hailed as global model in disaster management, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Malaysia has emerged as a global model in disaster management, with international powers seeking to learn from its expertise and specialised response teams, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. “I believe Malaysia has become a reference point in disaster management. “In disaster response, every second matters.

items.title: Police nab 32-year-old woman after body of foreign man discovered in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 24 — Police have detained a foreign woman to assist in investigations following the discovery of a man’s body at a residence in Klang, near here, yesterday. North Klang district police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao confirmed the incident and said police received information on the discovery of the body of a foreign man at 3.15pm at the residence. He said the 32-year-old woman was arrested following the discovery, and the case was being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Ipoh High Court vacates proceedings on Indira Gandhi’s bid to cite IGP for contempt
items.summary: IPOH, April 24 — The High Court in Ipoh has postponed proceedings on whether to grant M. Indira Gandhi leave to initiate contempt of court action against the Inspector-General of Police (IGP). The case has been vacated by the court,” he said. The High Court had been scheduled to deliver its decision today on whether to allow Indira to proceed with the contempt application against the IGP.

items.title: Global Sumud Flotilla swells as ships converge in Italy’s Siracusa en route to Gaza
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) is entering a new phase of escalation as vessels from Barcelona and across Italy converge at Marina di Siracusa on Thursday, forming an expanded and unified fleet ahead of its continued journey towards Gaza. In a statement today, GSF said that with 25 additional boats joining in Italy, the flotilla now exceeds the size of last year’s entire mission, signalling growing international participation and intensifying civilian pressure to challenge Israel’s illegal siege on Gaza. “With two additional ports still ahead, the mission continues to build in scale, coordination and global visibility.

items.title: Rescuers race to reach Australian hiker after fall on Mount Santubong descent
items.summary: Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said in a statement this morning that the victim, Belinda Lapier, fell on her way down from the summit. “Rescue teams from fire services and rangers headed to the victim’s last known location. “The victim said she was safe but having trouble getting out,” it said, adding that rocks, large trees, and darkness are blocking access to the victim.

items.title: Court reinstates 17-year jail term for former fish breeder over samurai sword robbery spree in Perak
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 24 — A former fish breeder who committed armed robberies over four consecutive days while armed with dangerous weapons, including a samurai sword, will serve 17 years in jail and receive seven strokes of the rotan. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Noorin Badaruddin, Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz and Datuk Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid yesterday allowed the prosecution’s appeal to reinstate the Sessions Court’s decision to impose 17 years’ imprisonment and seven strokes of the rotan on Tengku Izwan Shah Tengku Azman. The court overturned the High Court’s decision, which had reduced Tengku Izwan’s sentence from 17 years to 11 years.

items.title: Tangkap Azam Baki rally brings DAP, MCA youth together over MACC chief row
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The youth wings of rival political parties DAP Socialist Youth (Dapsy) and MCA Youth are set to join the April 25 “Tangkap Azam Baki” rally, in an unusual display of unity over the leadership of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission. According to The Edge , both groups said they would attend independently rather than as a formal alliance, while organisers said the gathering is aimed at demanding accountability and justice. MCA Youth separately said it was joining the rally out of concern over issues of public interest.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines rolls out Mumbai Indians-themed jet, taking IPL side’s colours global
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Malaysia Airlines has unveiled a special aircraft livery in collaboration with Indian Premier League side Mumbai Indians, bringing the cricket franchise’s signature blue and gold to the skies. Malaysia Airlines said the aircraft reflects the team’s growing global fanbase while reinforcing the airline’s role in connecting travellers through shared cultural and sporting experiences. Malaysia Aviation Group’s airline business CEO Bryan Foong said India remains a key growth market, with the collaboration aimed at strengthening regional connectivity and brand presence.
